title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost-management SaaS company backed by $85M+ in funding, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M ARR, deep Salesforce expertise, and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds 7+ requirement, deep Salesforce expertise, proven GTM scaling background, and fintech domain knowledge align perfectly with Finout's cloud cost-management focus. Tel Aviv hybrid location matches primary geographic preferences.
ai_opener: Hi [Name], I've been following Finout's growth trajectory in cloud cost optimization—a space that demands sophisticated GTM operations as you scale. With 15+ years building RevOps functions across B2B SaaS and fintech, including scaling beyond $30M ARR and deep Salesforce expertise, I'm particularly drawn to the opportunity to build Finout's operational backbone alongside your CRO and help accelerate your expansion into new markets.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager to serve as a key partner for Sales and RevOps, providing dashboards, reporting tools, and regional insights to optimize territory performance. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance on territory composition, and maintaining mapping in Salesforce/Fullcast. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool), and CRM proficiency. Salary range $149,000-$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's core RevOps expertise—territory strategy, Salesforce proficiency, B2B SaaS GTM optimization, and data analytics are directly in his wheelhouse. Remote US role removes any location friction, and the strategic scope (global scalability, cross-functional partnerships) matches his senior-level experience perfectly.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS and just saw Gong's RevOps Manager opening. Territory strategy and pipeline optimization are exactly what I've done at scale: redesigning models for growth, embedding analytics into sales ops, and partnering cross-functionally to drive predictable revenue. Your focus on regional insights and Salesforce/Fullcast optimization feels like a natural next chapter.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
description: Flex seeks a Revenue Operations Manager - Systems to build and own the Salesforce environment for its New Verticals division, designing revenue tech stack integrations and architecting the sales process from lead-to-close. Ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity and has stood up RevOps infrastructure before. Requires 5+ years in Revenue/Sales/GTM Operations with 2+ in a senior capacity, deep Salesforce expertise, and familiarity with GTM systems like Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot.
score_reason: Excellent match - senior RevOps executive with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience directly aligns with building Salesforce infrastructure, GTM systems integration, and sales process architecture. Remote US role fits location preferences, and deep expertise in Salesforce, HubSpot, and GTM tech stack exceeds requirements.
ai_opener: Hi there - I noticed Flex is building out RevOps infrastructure for New Verticals from the ground up. I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue tech stacks and Salesforce environments across hypergrowth B2B SaaS companies, and have specifically led implementations with Outreach, LeanData, and Clay in similar scale/complexity scenarios. I'd be interested in exploring how I could help you design a system that scales with your division's growth.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research used by brands like Unilever and Google, seeks a GTM Engineer to code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations. The role replaces patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents. Ideal for someone who can build dangerously effective GTM systems as the company scales 20x.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM automation, lead scoring, and pipeline systems—core RevOps competencies—but the role emphasizes coding/shipping custom tools over traditional RevOps strategy and process design. Remote location is ideal, though the company's B2C research focus (Unilever, Google) is outside Omri's sweet spot of B2B SaaS and Fintech.
ai_opener: Conveo's vision of replacing manual GTM workflows with AI-powered pipelines and lead scoring models directly mirrors the automation-first approach I've built at scale in B2B SaaS. Your 20x growth ambition needs someone who can translate RevOps strategy into shipping—I've architected Salesforce/HubSpot systems that became the nervous system for revenue teams. The question is whether you need a RevOps strategist to own GTM outcomes, or a builder focused purely on tool engineering.

title: GTM Engineer

The role focuses on technical tooling, workflow automation, and AI-powered integrations across the GTM stack. Ideal candidates combine engineering skills with go-to-market knowledge, can build internal tools and data pipelines, and are comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-growth startup environment building operational infrastructure from the ground up.

With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM operations and deep expertise in sales/marketing automation and data pipeline design, I'm excited about the opportunity to architect the operational systems that'll power your growth engine—especially combining RevOps strategy with the technical tooling and integrations that most operators leave on the table.
